THOUGHT:
Currently, I have citations covering corruption robustness (Hendrycks & Dietterich, 2019) and the theoretical concept of shortcut learning (Geirhos et al., 2020). However, I am missing a foundational reference that specifically addresses natural, in-the-wild distribution shifts across diverse real-world domains, which is critical for discussing deployment failures and dataset biases. The WILDS benchmark paper by Koh et al. (2021) perfectly fills this gap. It will be used to summarize research on natural distribution shifts, introduce key real-world datasets, highlight the limitations of standard i.i.d. benchmarks, compare empirical findings across domains, and support arguments for adopting robust, domain-aware evaluation methodologies.
